candidates.title: Luxury E-commerce Brand Visuals
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for scalable vector artwork and layered/retouched assets
candidates.brief: I’m creating a high-end brand that unites graphic design, videography, photography and a streamlined e-commerce website. The entire identity must feel unmistakably premium: think a deep black canvas paired with crisp white typography, minimal ornamentation, and a confident, modern layout. Logo & Core Identity The logo has to rely purely on typography—no symbols, icons or complex marks. Clean letterforms, balanced kerning and a subtle nod to luxury brands such as Saint Laurent or Balenciaga are key. Alongside the primary lock-up, I’ll need a monochrome palette, font recommendations and usage rules so every asset—from product packaging to video title cards—stays consistent. E-commerce Experience The site’s main job is selling. It should showcase products elegantly while highlighting verified customer reviews (this is the one essential feature I specified). candidates.title: Refreshing AVINSTAL Logo & Brand Identity
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Animate — for scalable vector artwork and multi-page layout
candidates.brief: Logo Redesign & Brand Refresh for AVINSTAL – Projection Mapping & AV Technology Company ## About Us AVINSTAL is a technology company with more than 10 years of experience specializing in: - Architectural projection mapping - 3D video mapping on buildings and structures - Professional projection systems - Audio-Video system integration - Multimedia installations - Event technology solutions Over the years, our company has evolved significantly, and we are now looking to modernize our visual identity with a professional logo redesign that better reflects our expertise, innovation, and future direction. Our current logo is attached for reference. --- ## Project Objective We are not looking for a completely unrelated new logo. candidates.title: Minimal Black Wordmark Logo
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for scalable vector artwork and multi-page layout
candidates.brief: I’m refreshing my brand, In&Row, and I’m looking for a designer to create a clean, modern, typography-focused logo that feels elevated, timeless, and versatile. Creative Direction The logo should be word-only and rely entirely on beautiful typography. Requirements: * Pure black logo (with a white/inverted version for dark backgrounds) * No icons, symbols, shapes, frames, underlines, flourishes, or decorative elements * Minimal, refined, and modern * Strong attention to spacing, kerning, balance, and visual weight * Must work equally well on packaging, website headers, social media, printed materials, and product labels Concepts I’d Like to Explore Please provide multiple typography directions, such as: Option A — Modern Refined Sans * Clean, airy, contemporary * Luxury without feeling trendy Option B — Sophisticated Serif * Confident, editorial, timeless * High-end but approachable Layou

candidates.title: Modern Monochrome Logo Design
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator — for scalable vector artwork
candidates.brief: I need a fresh brand mark that feels distinctly modern, sticks to a clean monochrome palette, and relies on crisp geometric shapes rather than illustrations or ornate flourishes. The end result should work just as well reversed out on dark backgrounds as it does in black on white, scale cleanly from social-media avatar size to large signage, and arrive to me in fully editable vector format (AI or SVG) alongside high-resolution PNGs. I have not locked down any exact shape combinations, so you’re welcome to experiment with negative space, symmetry, or subtle line-weight variations so long as the overall look remains contemporary and uncluttered. Bold yet simple shapes that can be recognized instantly are the priority; gradients, vintage treatments, or multicolored palettes are out of scope for this brief. candidates.title: Thumbnail & Editorial Image Designer Needed for Newsletter
candidates.brief: Upwork · Fixed price I’m looking for a talented thumbnail designer / graphic designer to help create a consistent visual style for my business newsletter. Each email will need one main hero thumbnail plus 3–4 additional supporting images based on the content inside the issue. The style I’m looking for is similar to Vox-style editorial explainer graphics: textured paper backgrounds, grayscale cutout photography, bold typography, yellow highlight blocks, hand-drawn arrows, charts, documents, collage elements, grid lines, halftone texture, and smart visual storytelling. The graphics should feel like a mix of a documentary explainer, investigative magazine cover, and scroll-stopping YouTube thumbnail.
